sources.name: CoinDesk RSS
sources.rate_limit: None observed/documented; poll every 1-5 min is safe
sources.sample_response_snippet: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <rss xmlns:content="http://purl.org/rss/1.0/modules/content/" ... <item><title><![CDATA[Crypto tax bills a work-in-progress as U.S. House lawmakers pose concerns]]></title><link>https://www.coindesk.com/policy/2026/06/09/crypto-tax-bills...</link><pubDate>Tue, 09 Jun 2026 21:06:01 +0000</pubDate>
sources.notes: 200 with browser UA. 25 items, latest <1 day old (Tue 09 Jun 21:06 UTC at fetch on Jun 10). HEADLINE+SUMMARY ONLY: description avg 138 chars, no usable content:encoded body. Article URLs are clean and server-rendered; fetch the <link> for full text. Includes media:content image.
sources.value_for_decisions: Highest-credibility breaking news (regulation, ETF, institutional flows, hacks). Sonnet should extract: catalyst type (regulation/ETF/hack/macro/partnership), coins named in title+description, direction (bullish/bearish framing), recency from pubDate. Magnitude usually low-medium per article; weight by section in URL path (/policy/, /markets/).

sources.name: CoinTelegraph RSS
sources.rate_limit: None observed; standard politeness (>=1 min poll)
sources.sample_response_snippet: ETH crash to $1K looms if key support breaks: Will futures traders step in?Tue, 09 Jun 2026 21:21:47 +0000https://cointelegraph.com/markets/ether-risks-revisit-of-1000-if-this-support-level-breaks-amid-leverage-reset-data
sources.notes: 200, application/xml. 30 items, latest <1 day old. Headline + ~144-char text summary (description is HTML with an inline image; strip tags). The clean canonical URL is in <guid>, the <link> carries utm params. High volume, includes price-analysis pieces (useful direction language: 'crash', 'rally', support levels).
sources.value_for_decisions: Highest-volume general feed; good for per-coin sentiment sweeps. Extract: ticker from title, direction words (crash/rally/surge/dump), price levels mentioned, catalyst type, pubDate recency. Discount sponsored/'partner' items (utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound in link).

sources.name: The Block RSS
sources.notes: 200, text/xml. 18 items, latest <1 day old (Tue 09 Jun 20:31 -0400). content:encoded exists but is only a ~150-char summary, NOT full text. Site articles are sometimes paywalled ('The Block Pro'), so the RSS summary may be all you get for some items.
sources.value_for_decisions: Institutional/funding/exchange news with fast turnaround. Extract: catalyst type (funding round, exchange incident, listing, regulatory), entities/coins from title, recency. Treat as high-credibility confirmation source rather than full-text reading.

sources.name: Decrypt RSS
sources.sample_response_snippet: <item><title>Crypto Tax Bills Face Pushback in House Committee Hearing</title><link>https://decrypt.co/370599/crypto-tax-bills-pushback-house-committee</link><pubDate>Tue, 09 Jun 2026 21:44:38 +0000</pubDate><description>Pro-crypto Democrats questioned staking and mining tax exemptions Tuesday...</description><dc:creator>Sander Lutz</dc:creator>
sources.notes: 200. 33 items, latest <1 day old (freshest of the six at fetch time). Headline + plain-text 1-2 sentence description (avg 130 chars), clean direct article URLs (server-rendered, easy to fetch full text with a browser UA).
sources.value_for_decisions: Fast general coverage incl. memecoins/NFT/gaming tokens others skip. Extract: coins, catalyst type, one-sentence summary is usually information-dense enough to classify direction without fetching the article.

sources.name: Bitcoin Magazine RSS (FULL TEXT)
sources.rate_limit: None observed; WordPress feed, poll >=5 min (only 10 items, slower cadence)
sources.notes: 200. 10 items, latest <1 day old. The ONLY feed of the six with full article text in-feed: content:encoded averages ~3,800 chars of body text (max 5,655), plus ~1,000-char description. BTC-centric editorial slant (bullish bias).
sources.value_for_decisions: Full text in-feed means sonnet can read the entire article with zero extra fetches. Best for BTC-specific catalysts (mining, treasury companies, policy). Extract: catalyst, magnitude cues from body (dollar amounts, dates), but correct for the publication's structural BTC-bullish bias.

sources.name: The Defiant RSS
sources.notes: 200, application/rss+xml. NOTE the URL is /api/feed (not /feed). 100 items (deepest archive of the six), latest <1 day old. content:encoded is a ~330-char extended summary, not full text.
sources.value_for_decisions: Best DeFi/alt-L1 coverage: protocol exploits, governance, TVL shifts, airdrops — catalysts that move mid-cap perps tradable on Hyperliquid. Extract: protocol/token name, catalyst type (exploit=strong bearish, airdrop/upgrade=bullish), the 330-char summary usually contains the key number (amount hacked, TVL change).

sources.name: Google News RSS search (per-coin queries)
sources.rate_limit: Unofficial/undocumented; keep to ~1 req/2-3 s per IP and cache per query, or risk 429/captcha
sources.sample_response_snippet: <title>Large cryptocurrencies drop on Solana, Bitcoin declines - MarketWatch</title><link>https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitAFBVV95cUxQZjhpc2dyVXJkZlJYRjl6SklVc21xajZ6VVFoazFha1UtREw1eVduX2hVRlhGaklQNTNDdjdudWRTb0kwLVVqakZDZ1RJSXpIdUFOV1JRaFVFZXRaOHJSUXVZUUZnNWR3Ul9yX2lWTWF6cHF4VjhhTm9sa1N4RkFSdnVuUENvRkRjR2ZlbURPdmc1OWR1Wk1pVm1IRGtxQlRwSmt6SVkzSmd6YlY4dEJZRGpLdUg?oc=5</link>
sources.notes: Verified with q='solana crypto when:1d': 200, 100 items, items from the last 24h. Operators that work: when:1d / when:7d, quoted phrases, site:. CAVEAT: <link> is a Google-encoded redirect (rss/articles/CBMi...), not the real URL, and since 2024 it does NOT resolve with a simple HTTP redirect — use the title + <source> domain for attribution, or a decoder lib (e.g. googlenewsdecoder, which uses Google's batchexecute endpoint, also keyless). Title format is 'Headline - Publisher'.
sources.value_for_decisions: The per-coin recall sweep: one query per held/candidate symbol. Extract: event clustering (many publishers on same story within hours = real catalyst, magnitude proxy), publisher credibility from <source>, direction from headline language, recency from pubDate. Headlines only — treat as a detector, then read the story via the publisher's own feed/site.

sources.name: CryptoPanic API v2 (developer tier)
sources.rate_limit: Plan-based: per-second limit + monthly cap (403/429 on breach); free developer tier quota shown after signup at cryptopanic.com/developers/api/keys
sources.sample_response_snippet: (keyless probe of https://cryptopanic.com/api/developer/v2/posts/ returned HTTP 404 with an HTML shell: '<!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en"><head><meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">...' — endpoint requires auth_token; could not verify 200 shape without a key)
sources.notes: Base URL pattern is https://cryptopanic.com/api/{PLAN}/v2/ (plan=developer for the free tier). Keyless returns 404, so verified=false. Their web 'RSS' (cryptopanic.com/news/rss/) returned an HTML Vue app, NOT a feed — do not use. Docs pages are JS-rendered/Cloudflare-fronted (curl gets a 13KB shell). Free key signup is instant.
sources.value_for_decisions: Only worth it as a fallback: pre-tagged coins (currencies field), community votes (positive/negative/important) as a crowd-sourced direction+magnitude proxy, and filter=hot for what is moving now. Extract: currencies array, vote ratios, panic_score if present, source domain.

sources.name: Binance CMS announcements JSON (new listings, catalogId=48)
sources.rate_limit: Unofficial bapi endpoint, no documented limit; aggressive polling trips Binance WAF/captcha — poll ~1/min with a browser UA
sources.notes: YES, still works keyless from this US machine (200, browser UA). Freshest article releaseDate 2026-06-09T06:15 UTC ('Binance Futures Will Launch Multiple USD(S)-Margined TradFi Perpetual Contracts'). The legacy composite variant ALSO still works: /bapi/composite/v1/public/cms/article/catalog/list/query?catalogId=48&pageNo=1&pageSize=5 (200, same articles). The article-detail endpoint (/apex/cms/article/detail/query?articleCode=...) returns 200 but data:null — full body is NOT retrievable via API; titles are information-complete for listings anyway. Other catalogIds: 49=delistings, 161=futures/derivatives.
sources.value_for_decisions: THE highest-alpha source here: Binance futures/spot listing titles name the exact ticker and effective date. Extract with a regex pass before any LLM: /([A-Z0-9]{2,10})USDT (Perpetual|Contract)/ or 'Will List X (TICKER)'. Catalyst=listing (bullish for the listed coin, minutes-level urgency via releaseDate epoch ms); delisting catalog = strong bearish. Diff article ids between polls.

sources.name: Bybit v5 announcements API (new_crypto)
sources.rate_limit: No rate headers returned; Bybit docs: public endpoints IP-limited (~600 req/5s shared) — poll 1/min is trivial
sources.sample_response_snippet: {"retCode":0,"retMsg":"OK","result":{"total":1518,"list":[{"title":"New listing: AAOIUSDT Perpetual Contract, with up to 10x leverage","description":"Bybit has listed the AAOIUSDT Perpetual Contract. Trading is now open with up to 10x leverage.","type":{"title":"New Listings","key":"new_crypto"},"tags":["Derivatives"],"url":"https://announcements.bybit.com/en-US/article/new-listing-aaoiusdt-perpet
sources.notes: Official, documented, keyless, clean JSON with title+description+tags+dateTimestamp. type values: new_crypto, delistings, latest_activities, etc. Easiest exchange feed to parse — description is a complete sentence with ticker and leverage.
sources.value_for_decisions: Listing catalyst with structured fields: parse ticker from title ('New listing: XUSDT Perpetual'), tags=['Derivatives'] distinguishes perp vs spot, dateTimestamp for recency. Bybit listings often precede/echo Binance ones — cross-exchange listing momentum signal.

sources.name: OKX support announcements API (new listings)
sources.rate_limit: No headers observed; OKX docs list 5 req/2s for this endpoint
sources.sample_response_snippet: {"code":"0","msg":"","data":[{"details":[{"annType":"announcements-new-listings","title":"OKX will launch PROS/USD and PROS/EUR for spot trading","url":"https://www.okx.com/en-eu/help/okx-will-launch-pros-usd-and-pros-eur-for-spot-trading","pTime":"1778644810930","businessPTime":"1778644800000"},{"annType":"announcements-new-listings","title":"OKX to list KAT and OKB on spot in EEA","url":"https:/
sources.notes: Official, documented, keyless. annType filters: announcements-new-listings, announcements-delistings, etc. pTime/businessPTime are epoch ms (businessPTime = when the listing takes effect — useful for pre-positioning).
sources.value_for_decisions: Third confirmation leg for listing momentum. Extract ticker(s) from title, spot vs perp from title wording, and businessPTime as the exact go-live timestamp (catalyst with a known future time = schedulable trade).

sources.name: Upbit announcements API (trade/listing category)
sources.rate_limit: No headers observed; unofficial-but-stable web API, poll <=1/30s with browser UA
sources.notes: 200 keyless from US IP. Titles are Korean but the TICKER IS ALWAYS IN LATIN PARENS — e.g. '(CTR)' — so a regex \(([A-Z0-9]{2,8})\) extracts the coin with no translation needed. Latest notice was a brand-new listing <1 day old at fetch time. The 'Upbit pump' (KRW retail flow) is one of the most reliable listing-pop phenomena in crypto.
sources.value_for_decisions: Highest single-event magnitude of all listing sources: Upbit KRW listings historically pop the coin double digits in minutes. Extract: ticker from parens, market (BTC/USDT/KRW in title), listed_at recency. If the coin has a Hyperliquid perp, an Upbit listing notice seconds old is an immediate long candidate; staleness >30 min means the move already happened.

sources.name: Hyperliquid perp universe (listing/delisting detector)
sources.rate_limit: Documented: 1200 request-weight/min per IP on /info (meta is cheap); polling 1/min uses a fraction
sources.notes: Hyperliquid has NO machine-readable announcement feed: listings are announced on X (@HyperliquidX) and Discord only; the GitBook docs (hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs, verified 200) are HTML with no RSS, and free X/nitter mirrors are dead/unreliable. The deterministic substitute: diff the 'universe' array between polls — a new 'name' = new perp listed; 'isDelisted':true appearing = delist. Same call works on the testnet host the project already uses.
sources.value_for_decisions: Ground truth for what is tradable in this project's paper-trading layer. New universe entry = listing catalyst on HL itself (early listings often pump on HL-native flow); maxLeverage tells the engine its sizing cap immediately; isDelisted = forced-exit signal for any open paper position. No LLM needed — pure set-diff, then sonnet only interprets cross-source context.

sources.name: CoinMarketCal events API
sources.rate_limit: Free plan: limited monthly call quota and events restricted to ~7 days ahead (exact quota shown after signup); paid from ~$49.80/mo for unlimited
sources.sample_response_snippet: (keyless probe returned HTTP 403: '{"status": {"error_message":"Forbidden","error_code": 403}}' — endpoint live and JSON-shaped but requires x-api-key header; could not verify 200 body without a key)
sources.notes: Auth is an x-api-key header from coinmarketcal.com/en/api (the signup page itself is Cloudflare-403 to curl; use a real browser to register). Marked verified=false per the rules. Events are community-submitted and voted, with proof links.
sources.value_for_decisions: The only forward-looking source in this list: scheduled catalysts (mainnet launches, token unlocks, burns, conference talks) days before they hit news feeds. Extract: coins[] (native field), date_event, categories, vote/confidence percentage as magnitude prior. Token unlocks = bearish; mainnet/burn = bullish. Use the 7-day free window as a pre-positioning calendar; skip if key friction is too high — Google News 'when:7d' per coin is a weaker free substitute.

summary: All endpoints were fetched live from this machine on 2026-06-10 (curl.exe, browser UA, no auth keys). 12 of 14 verified with real 200s and expected shapes; the 2 unverified (CryptoPanic, CoinMarketCal) require free keys and returned the expected auth-rejection responses keyless. KEY FINDINGS: (1) All six publisher RSS feeds work today and are fresh (<24h items): CoinDesk /arc/outboundfeeds/rss/, CoinTelegraph /rss, The Block /rss.xml, Decrypt /feed, Bitcoin Magazine /feed, The Defiant /api/feed (note the /api/ prefix). Full-text reality: ONLY Bitcoin Magazine ships full article bodies in-feed (~3.8k chars avg); The Defiant gives ~330-char extended summaries; the rest are headline + 130-180-char blurbs, so full reading requires fetching the article URL (easy for Decrypt/CoinDesk/CoinTelegraph, sometimes paywalled on The Block). (2) Google News RSS search works (100 items/query, when:1d operator) but links are Google-encoded redirects that no longer resolve via plain HTTP — use title + <source> publisher for triage, or a batchexecute-based decoder. (3) Binance CMS announcement JSON still works KEYLESS from a US IP on BOTH the apex and legacy composite paths; catalogId=48 = new listings (freshest item <24h, a futures-listing notice); the article-detail endpoint returns data:null so titles are all you get (sufficient for listings). (4) Bybit v5 and OKX v5 announcement APIs are official, documented, keyless, and the cleanest to parse; Upbit's JSON works from a US IP with tickers extractable from Latin parens in Korean titles — the single highest-magnitude listing signal. (5) Hyperliquid has no feed at all (X/Discord only; GitBook is HTML) — the reliable machine path is diffing POST /info {"type":"meta"} universe between polls for listings/delists, which doubles as ground truth for the project's paper-trading layer. (6) Recommended free stack for a sonnet-grade agent: regex-first listing detectors (Binance/Bybit/OKX/Upbit/HL-diff, no LLM needed for extraction), RSS headlines for catalyst classification (type, coins, direction, recency), Google News per-coin sweeps for event-cluster magnitude, with CryptoPanic/CoinMarketCal as optional free-key fallbacks for pre-tagged sentiment and forward calendars. No rate-limit headers were observed anywhere; documented/safe polling: OKX 5 req/2s, Hyperliquid 1200 weight/min, everything else politeness-limited (~1/min is safe across the board).
